mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth over a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp in 3 months, or the signal's predictive power is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, it signals an accommodative monetary policy despite inflationary pressures, which can lead to asset price bubbles and eventual negative S&P 500 forward returns as policy tightens over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently positive when CPIAUCSL is high and FEDFUNDS is low, or the interaction term shows no predictive power (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an increase in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strengthening manufacturing and supply chains, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery regimes, positive INDPRO growth does not lead to positive SPX 5-day returns more than 55% of the time, or the signal's accuracy is below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening construction activity and broader economic demand, which foreshadows slower economic growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% MoM, or the signal's negative predictive power is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals a robust labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth exceeds 150K MoM, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations, or the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a tightening monetary policy, which raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns in the short term (5 trading days).
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a FEDFUNDS increase of > 0.25pp MoM, or the signal's negative direction is not confirmed.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but industrial production (INDPRO) is declining, it suggests a divergence where demand outstrips supply capacity, potentially leading to inflationary pressures and eventual economic overheating, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% YoY AND INDPRO growth < 0% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently positive when GDPC1 growth is strong and INDPRO is contracting, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance (p-value > 0.1).
